revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Udozuka Kofun | native_name =烏土塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Udozuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Udozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Udozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|37|4.07|N|135|42|8.67|E|region:JP-29|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Heguri, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|37|4.07|N|135|42|8.67|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|''' Udozuka Kofun'''|烏土塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|burial mound]], located in the Kasugaoka neighborhood of the town of [[Heguri, Nara]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1971.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/217109|title=烏土塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Udozuka Kofun is located at the tip of a branching ridge at the eastern foot of the Ikoma Mountains, which run north and south along the border between [[Osaka Prefecture]] and [[Nara Prefecture]]. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were carried out in 1969 . It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, with a total length of 60.5 meters, making it the largest tumulus in the Heguri region. The posterior circular portion has a diameter of about 35 meters and height of about 9 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular portion is 31 meters. The mound was constructed in one tier, although no trace of ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones have been found, the mound had a row of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. A moat (approximately 2 meters wide) has been found around the mound.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The horizontal-entry stone [[burial chamber]] opens to the south of the rear circular mound was built with huge stones. The burial chamber is about 6 meters long, 2.1 meters wide, and 4.4 meters high. The entrance passage is about 8.2 meters long and 2.1 meters wide, with a culvert-type drainage ditch in the center. A house-shaped stone sarcophagus made of [[tuff]] is located in the burial chamber, and the bottom of another tuff sarcophagus is located in the entrance passage. Excavated [[grave goods]] include a four-animal [[bronze mirror]], a large iron sword, gilt bronze horse equipment, jewels, and [[Sue ware]] pottry. It is believed to have been built around the mid-6th century in the late Kofun period, and is considered a rare example of a megalithic tomb with clearly excavated artifacts.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a five-minute walk from [[Tatsutagawa Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Ikoma Line]].
<gallery> Udozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial Chamber Udozuka Kofun, genshitsu-3.jpg|Burial chamber looking towards entrance 烏土塚古墳出土 巫女埴輪.JPG| haniwa in form of a woman 烏土塚古墳 出土品.JPG|grave goods </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:kk]][[Category:-inline}} *[https://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/soshiki/14/1081.html Heguri Town home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.library.pref.nara.jp/nara_2010/0097.html Nara Prefectural Library]{{in lang|ja}}

A house-shaped stone sarcophagus made of [[tuff]] is located in the burial chamber, and the bottom of another tuff sarcophagus is located in the entrance passage. Excavated [[grave goods]] include a four-animal [[bronze mirror]], a large iron sword, gilt bronze horse equipment, jewels, and [[Sue ware]] pottry. It is believed to have been built around the mid-6th century in the late Kofun period, and is considered a rare example of a megalithic tomb with clearly excavated artifacts.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a five-minute walk from [[Tatsutagawa Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Ikoma Line]].

{{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Udozuka Kofun | native_name =烏土塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Udozuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Udozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Udozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|37|4.07|N|135|42|8.67|E|region:JP-29|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Heguri, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|37|4.07|N|135|42|8.67|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|''' Udozuka Kofun'''|烏土塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|burial mound]], located in the Kasugaoka neighborhood of the town of [[Heguri, Nara]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1971.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/217109|title=烏土塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Udozuka Kofun is located at the tip of a branching ridge at the eastern foot of the Ikoma Mountains, which run north and south along the border between [[Osaka Prefecture]] and [[Nara Prefecture]]. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were carried out in 1969 . It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, with a total length of 60.5 meters, making it the largest tumulus in the Heguri region. The posterior circular portion has a diameter of about 35 meters and height of about 9 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular portion is 31 meters. The mound was constructed in one tier, although no trace of ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones have been found, the mound had a row of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. A moat (approximately 2 meters wide) has been found around the mound.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The horizontal-entry stone [[burial chamber]] opens to the south of the rear circular mound was built with huge stones. The burial chamber is about 6 meters long, 2.1 meters wide, and 4.4 meters high. The entrance passage is about 8.2 meters long and 2.1 meters wide, with a culvert-type drainage ditch in the center. A house-shaped stone sarcophagus made of [[tuff]] is located in the burial chamber, and the bottom of another tuff sarcophagus is located in the entrance passage. Excavated [[grave goods]] include a four-animal [[bronze mirror]], a large iron sword, gilt bronze horse equipment, jewels, and [[Sue ware]] pottry. It is believed to have been built around the mid-6th century in the late Kofun period, and is considered a rare example of a megalithic tomb with clearly excavated artifacts.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a five-minute walk from [[Tatsutagawa Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Ikoma Line]].
<gallery> Udozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial Chamber Udozuka Kofun, genshitsu-3.jpg|Burial chamber looking towards entrance 烏土塚古墳出土 巫女埴輪.JPG| haniwa in form of a woman 烏土塚古墳 出土品.JPG|grave goods </gallery>

{{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped burial mound in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Udozuka Kofun | native_name =烏土塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Udozuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Udozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Udozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|37|4.07|N|135|42|8.67|E|region:JP-29|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Heguri, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|37|4.07|N|135|42|8.67|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|''' Udozuka Kofun'''|烏土塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|burial mound]], located in the Kasugaoka neighborhood of the town of [[Heguri, Nara]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1971.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/217109|title=烏土塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Udozuka Kofun is located at the tip of a branching ridge at the eastern foot of the Ikoma Mountains, which run north and south along the border between [[Osaka Prefecture]] and [[Nara Prefecture]]. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were carried out in 1969 . It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, with a total length of 60.5 meters, making it the largest tumulus in the Heguri region. The posterior circular portion has a diameter of about 35 meters and height of about 9 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular portion is 31 meters. The mound was constructed in one tier, although no trace of ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones have been found, the mound had a row of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. A moat (approximately 2 meters wide) has been found around the mound.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The horizontal-entry stone [[burial chamber]] opens to the south of the rear circular mound was built with huge stones. The burial chamber is about 6 meters long, 2.1 meters wide, and 4.4 meters high. The entrance passage is about 8.2 meters long and 2.1 meters wide, with a culvert-type drainage ditch in the center. A house-shaped stone sarcophagus made of [[tuff]] is located in the burial chamber, and the bottom of another tuff sarcophagus is located in the entrance passage. Excavated [[grave goods]] include a four-animal [[bronze mirror]], a large iron sword, gilt bronze horse equipment, jewels, and [[Sue ware]] pottry. It is believed to have been built around the mid-6th century in the late Kofun period, and is considered a rare example of a megalithic tomb with clearly excavated artifacts.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a five-minute walk from [[Tatsutagawa Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Ikoma Line]].
<gallery> Udozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial Chamber Udozuka Kofun, genshitsu-3.jpg|Burial chamber looking towards entrance 烏土塚古墳出土 巫女埴輪.JPG| haniwa in form of a woman 烏土塚古墳 出土品.JPG|grave goods </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]

{{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Udozuka Kofun | native_name =烏土塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Udozuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Udozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Udozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|37|4.07|N|135|42|8.67|E|region:JP-29|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Heguri, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|37|4.07|N|135|42|8.67|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|''' Udozuka Kofun'''|烏土塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kasugaoka neighborhood of the town of [[Heguri, Nara]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1971.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/217109|title=烏土塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Udozuka Kofun is located at the tip of a branching ridge at the eastern foot of the Ikoma Mountains, which run north and south along the border between [[Osaka Prefecture]] and [[Nara Prefecture]]. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were carried out in 1969 . It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, with a total length of 60.5 meters, making it the largest tumulus in the Heguri region. The posterior circular portion has a diameter of about 35 meters and height of about 9 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular portion is 31 meters. The mound was constructed in one tier, although no trace of ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones have been found, the mound had a row of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. A moat (approximately 2 meters wide) has been found around the mound.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The horizontal-entry stone [[burial chamber]] opens to the south of the rear circular mound was built with huge stones. The burial chamber is about 6 meters long, 2.1 meters wide, and 4.4 meters high. The entrance passage is about 8.2 meters long and 2.1 meters wide, with a culvert-type drainage ditch in the center. A house-shaped stone sarcophagus made of [[tuff]] is located in the burial chamber, and the bottom of another tuff sarcophagus is located in the entrance passage. Excavated [[grave goods]] include a four-animal [[bronze mirror]], a large iron sword, gilt bronze horse equipment, jewels, and [[Sue ware]] pottry. It is believed to have been built around the mid-6th century in the late Kofun period, and is considered a rare example of a megalithic tomb with clearly excavated artifacts.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a five-minute walk from [[Tatsutagawa Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Ikoma Line]].
<gallery> Udozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial Chamber Udozuka Kofun, genshitsu-3.jpg|Burial chamber looking towards entrance 烏土塚古墳出土 巫女埴輪.JPG| haniwa in form of a woman 烏土塚古墳 出土品.JPG|grave goods </gallery>

It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, with a total length of 60.5 meters, making it the largest tumulus in the Heguri region. The posterior circular portion has a diameter of about 35 meters and height of about 9 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular portion is 31 meters. The mound was constructed in one tier, although no trace of ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones have been found, the mound had a row of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. A moat (approximately 2 meters wide) has been found around the mound.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The horizontal-entry stone [[burial chamber]] opens to the south of the rear circular mound was built with huge stones. The burial chamber is about 6 meters long, 2.1 meters wide, and 4.4 meters high. The entrance passage is about 8.2 meters long and 2.1 meters wide, with a culvert-type drainage ditch in the center. A house-shaped stone sarcophagus made of [[tuff]] is located in the burial chamber, and the bottom of another tuff sarcophagus is located in the entrance passage. Excavated [[grave goods]] include a four-animal [[bronze mirror]], a large iron sword, gilt bronze horse equipment, jewels, and [[Sue ware]] pottry. It is believed to have been built around the mid-6th century in the late Kofun period, and is considered a rare example of a megalithic tomb with clearly excavated artifacts.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a five-minute walk from [[Tatsutagawa Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Ikoma Line]].

{{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Udozuka Kofun | native_name =烏土塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Udozuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Udozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Udozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|34|37|4.07|N|135|42|8.67|E|region:JP-29|display=inline}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Heguri, Nara]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|37|4.07|N|135|42|8.67|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|''' Udozuka Kofun'''|烏土塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kasugaoka neighborhood of the town of [[Heguri, Nara]] in the [[Kansai region]] of [[Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1971.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/217109|title=烏土塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Udozuka Kofun is located at the tip of a branching ridge at the eastern foot of the Ikoma Mountains, which run north and south along the border between [[Osaka Prefecture]] and [[Nara Prefecture]]. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were carried out in 1969 . It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, with a total length of 60.5 meters, making it the largest tumulus in the Heguri region. The posterior circular portion has a diameter of about 35 meters and height of about 9 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular portion is 31 meters. The mound was constructed in one tier, although no trace of ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones have been found, the mound had a row of cylindrical ''[[haniwa]]''. A moat (approximately 2 meters wide) has been found around the mound.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The horizontal-entry stone [[burial chamber]] opens to the south of the rear circular mound was built with huge stones. The burial chamber is about 6 meters long, 2.1 meters wide, and 4.4 meters high. The entrance passage is about 8.2 meters long and 2.1 meters wide, with a culvert-type drainage ditch in the center. A house-shaped stone sarcophagus made of [[tuff]] is located in the burial chamber, and the bottom of another tuff sarcophagus is located in the entrance passage. Excavated [[grave goods]] include a four-animal [[bronze mirror]], a large iron sword, gilt bronze horse equipment, jewels, and [[Sue ware]] pottry. It is believed to have been built around the mid-6th century in the late Kofun period, and is considered a rare example of a megalithic tomb with clearly excavated artifacts.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a five-minute walk from [[Tatsutagawa Station]] on the [[Kintetsu Railway]] [[Ikoma Line]].
<gallery> Udozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial Chamber Udozuka Kofun, genshitsu-3.jpg|Burial chamber looking towards entrance 烏土塚古墳出土 巫女埴輪.JPG| haniwa in form of a woman 烏土塚古墳 出土品.JPG|grave goods </gallery>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Udozuka Kofun]][[en:Udozuka Kofun]][[ja:烏土塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q60988845]] Wikidata name: Udozuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Udozuka Kofun | native_name =烏土塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Udozuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Udozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Udozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.617797|N|135.702408|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Heguri, Nara|Heguri, Nara]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|37|4.07|N|135|42|8.67|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|''' Udozuka Kofun'''|烏土塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kasugaoka neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Heguri, Nara|Heguri, Nara]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1971.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/217109|title=烏土塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Udozuka Kofun is located at the tip of a branching ridge at the eastern foot of the Ikoma Mountains, which run north and south along the border between [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] and [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were carried out in 1969 . It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, with a total length of 60.5 meters, making it the largest tumulus in the Heguri region. The posterior circular portion has a diameter of about 35 meters and height of about 9 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular portion is 31 meters. The mound was constructed in one tier, although no trace of ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones have been found, the mound had a row of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]''. A moat (approximately 2 meters wide) has been found around the mound.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The horizontal-entry stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] opens to the south of the rear circular mound was built with huge stones. The burial chamber is about 6 meters long, 2.1 meters wide, and 4.4 meters high. The entrance passage is about 8.2 meters long and 2.1 meters wide, with a culvert-type drainage ditch in the center. A house-shaped stone sarcophagus made of [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] is located in the burial chamber, and the bottom of another tuff sarcophagus is located in the entrance passage. Excavated [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] include a four-animal [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]], a large iron sword, gilt bronze horse equipment, jewels, and [[Sue ware]] pottry. It is believed to have been built around the mid-6th century in the late Kofun period, and is considered a rare example of a megalithic tomb with clearly excavated artifacts.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a five-minute walk from [[:en:Tatsutagawa Station|Tatsutagawa Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[:en:Ikoma Line|Ikoma Line]].
<gallery> Udozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial Chamber Udozuka Kofun, genshitsu-3.jpg|Burial chamber looking towards entrance 烏土塚古墳出土 巫女埴輪.JPG| haniwa in form of a woman 烏土塚古墳 出土品.JPG|grave goods </gallery>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:-inline}} *[https://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/soshiki/14/1081.html Heguri Town home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.library.pref.nara.jp/nara_2010/0097.html Nara Prefectural Library]{{in lang|ja}}

It is believed to have been built around the mid-6th century in the late Kofun period, and is considered a rare example of a megalithic tomb with clearly excavated artifacts.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a five-minute walk from [[:en:Tatsutagawa Station|Tatsutagawa Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[:en:Ikoma Line|Ikoma Line]].

{{nihongo|''' Udozuka Kofun'''|烏土塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kasugaoka neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Heguri, Nara|Heguri, Nara]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1971.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/217109|title=烏土塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Udozuka Kofun is located at the tip of a branching ridge at the eastern foot of the Ikoma Mountains, which run north and south along the border between [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] and [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were carried out in 1969 . It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, with a total length of 60.5 meters, making it the largest tumulus in the Heguri region. The posterior circular portion has a diameter of about 35 meters and height of about 9 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular portion is 31 meters. The mound was constructed in one tier, although no trace of ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones have been found, the mound had a row of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]''. A moat (approximately 2 meters wide) has been found around the mound.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The horizontal-entry stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] opens to the south of the rear circular mound was built with huge stones. The burial chamber is about 6 meters long, 2.1 meters wide, and 4.4 meters high. The entrance passage is about 8.2 meters long and 2.1 meters wide, with a culvert-type drainage ditch in the center. A house-shaped stone sarcophagus made of [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] is located in the burial chamber, and the bottom of another tuff sarcophagus is located in the entrance passage. Excavated [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] include a four-animal [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]], a large iron sword, gilt bronze horse equipment, jewels, and [[Sue ware]] pottry. It is believed to have been built around the mid-6th century in the late Kofun period, and is considered a rare example of a megalithic tomb with clearly excavated artifacts.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a five-minute walk from [[:en:Tatsutagawa Station|Tatsutagawa Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[:en:Ikoma Line|Ikoma Line]].

It is believed to have been built around the mid-6th century in the late Kofun period, and is considered a rare example of a megalithic tomb with clearly excavated artifacts.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a five-minute walk from [[:en:Tatsutagawa Station|Tatsutagawa Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[:en:Ikoma Line|Ikoma Line]].

{{nihongo|''' Udozuka Kofun'''|烏土塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kasugaoka neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Heguri, Nara|Heguri, Nara]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1971.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/217109|title=烏土塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Udozuka Kofun is located at the tip of a branching ridge at the eastern foot of the Ikoma Mountains, which run north and south along the border between [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] and [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were carried out in 1969 . It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, with a total length of 60.5 meters, making it the largest tumulus in the Heguri region. The posterior circular portion has a diameter of about 35 meters and height of about 9 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular portion is 31 meters. The mound was constructed in one tier, although no trace of ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones have been found, the mound had a row of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]''. A moat (approximately 2 meters wide) has been found around the mound.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The horizontal-entry stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] opens to the south of the rear circular mound was built with huge stones. The burial chamber is about 6 meters long, 2.1 meters wide, and 4.4 meters high. The entrance passage is about 8.2 meters long and 2.1 meters wide, with a culvert-type drainage ditch in the center. A house-shaped stone sarcophagus made of [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] is located in the burial chamber, and the bottom of another tuff sarcophagus is located in the entrance passage. Excavated [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] include a four-animal [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]], a large iron sword, gilt bronze horse equipment, jewels, and [[Sue ware]] pottry. It is believed to have been built around the mid-6th century in the late Kofun period, and is considered a rare example of a megalithic tomb with clearly excavated artifacts.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a five-minute walk from [[:en:Tatsutagawa Station|Tatsutagawa Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[:en:Ikoma Line|Ikoma Line]].

It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, with a total length of 60.5 meters, making it the largest tumulus in the Heguri region. The posterior circular portion has a diameter of about 35 meters and height of about 9 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular portion is 31 meters. The mound was constructed in one tier, although no trace of ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones have been found, the mound had a row of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]''. A moat (approximately 2 meters wide) has been found around the mound.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The horizontal-entry stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] opens to the south of the rear circular mound was built with huge stones. The burial chamber is about 6 meters long, 2.1 meters wide, and 4.4 meters high. The entrance passage is about 8.2 meters long and 2.1 meters wide, with a culvert-type drainage ditch in the center. A house-shaped stone sarcophagus made of [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] is located in the burial chamber, and the bottom of another tuff sarcophagus is located in the entrance passage. Excavated [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] include a four-animal [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]], a large iron sword, gilt bronze horse equipment, jewels, and [[Sue ware]] pottry. It is believed to have been built around the mid-6th century in the late Kofun period, and is considered a rare example of a megalithic tomb with clearly excavated artifacts.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a five-minute walk from [[:en:Tatsutagawa Station|Tatsutagawa Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[:en:Ikoma Line|Ikoma Line]].
<gallery> Udozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial Chamber Udozuka Kofun, genshitsu-3.jpg|Burial chamber looking towards entrance 烏土塚古墳出土 巫女埴輪.JPG| haniwa in form of a woman 烏土塚古墳 出土品.JPG|grave goods </gallery>

{{nihongo|''' Udozuka Kofun'''|烏土塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kasugaoka neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Heguri, Nara|Heguri, Nara]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1971.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/217109|title=烏土塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Udozuka Kofun is located at the tip of a branching ridge at the eastern foot of the Ikoma Mountains, which run north and south along the border between [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] and [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were carried out in 1969 . It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, with a total length of 60.5 meters, making it the largest tumulus in the Heguri region. The posterior circular portion has a diameter of about 35 meters and height of about 9 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular portion is 31 meters. The mound was constructed in one tier, although no trace of ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones have been found, the mound had a row of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]''. A moat (approximately 2 meters wide) has been found around the mound.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The horizontal-entry stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] opens to the south of the rear circular mound was built with huge stones. The burial chamber is about 6 meters long, 2.1 meters wide, and 4.4 meters high. The entrance passage is about 8.2 meters long and 2.1 meters wide, with a culvert-type drainage ditch in the center. A house-shaped stone sarcophagus made of [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] is located in the burial chamber, and the bottom of another tuff sarcophagus is located in the entrance passage. Excavated [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] include a four-animal [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]], a large iron sword, gilt bronze horse equipment, jewels, and [[Sue ware]] pottry. It is believed to have been built around the mid-6th century in the late Kofun period, and is considered a rare example of a megalithic tomb with clearly excavated artifacts.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a five-minute walk from [[:en:Tatsutagawa Station|Tatsutagawa Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[:en:Ikoma Line|Ikoma Line]].

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Udozuka Kofun]][[en:Udozuka Kofun]][[ja:烏土塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[commons:Category:Udozuka Kofun]][[en:Udozuka Kofun]][[ja:烏土塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Udozuka Kofun]][[en:Udozuka Kofun]][[ja:烏土塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q60988845]] Wikidata name: Udozuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Udozuka Kofun | native_name =烏土塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Udozuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Udozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Udozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.617797|N|135.702408|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Heguri, Nara|Heguri, Nara]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|37|4.07|N|135|42|8.67|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|''' Udozuka Kofun'''|烏土塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kasugaoka neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Heguri, Nara|Heguri, Nara]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1971.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/217109|title=烏土塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Udozuka Kofun is located at the tip of a branching ridge at the eastern foot of the Ikoma Mountains, which run north and south along the border between [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] and [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were carried out in 1969 . It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, with a total length of 60.5 meters, making it the largest tumulus in the Heguri region. The posterior circular portion has a diameter of about 35 meters and height of about 9 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular portion is 31 meters. The mound was constructed in one tier, although no trace of ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones have been found, the mound had a row of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]''. A moat (approximately 2 meters wide) has been found around the mound.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The horizontal-entry stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] opens to the south of the rear circular mound was built with huge stones. The burial chamber is about 6 meters long, 2.1 meters wide, and 4.4 meters high. The entrance passage is about 8.2 meters long and 2.1 meters wide, with a culvert-type drainage ditch in the center. A house-shaped stone sarcophagus made of [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] is located in the burial chamber, and the bottom of another tuff sarcophagus is located in the entrance passage. Excavated [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] include a four-animal [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]], a large iron sword, gilt bronze horse equipment, jewels, and [[Sue ware]] pottry. It is believed to have been built around the mid-6th century in the late Kofun period, and is considered a rare example of a megalithic tomb with clearly excavated artifacts.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a five-minute walk from [[:en:Tatsutagawa Station|Tatsutagawa Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[:en:Ikoma Line|Ikoma Line]].
<gallery> Udozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial Chamber Udozuka Kofun, genshitsu-3.jpg|Burial chamber looking towards entrance 烏土塚古墳出土 巫女埴輪.JPG| haniwa in form of a woman 烏土塚古墳 出土品.JPG|grave goods </gallery>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)]]

{{nihongo|''' Udozuka Kofun'''|烏土塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kasugaoka neighborhood of the town of [[:en:Heguri, Nara|Heguri, Nara]] in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1971.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/217109|title=烏土塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Udozuka Kofun is located at the tip of a branching ridge at the eastern foot of the Ikoma Mountains, which run north and south along the border between [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]] and [[:en:Nara Prefecture|Nara Prefecture]]. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were carried out in 1969 . It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, with a total length of 60.5 meters, making it the largest tumulus in the Heguri region. The posterior circular portion has a diameter of about 35 meters and height of about 9 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular portion is 31 meters. The mound was constructed in one tier, although no trace of ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones have been found, the mound had a row of cylindrical ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]''. A moat (approximately 2 meters wide) has been found around the mound.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The horizontal-entry stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] opens to the south of the rear circular mound was built with huge stones. The burial chamber is about 6 meters long, 2.1 meters wide, and 4.4 meters high. The entrance passage is about 8.2 meters long and 2.1 meters wide, with a culvert-type drainage ditch in the center. A house-shaped stone sarcophagus made of [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] is located in the burial chamber, and the bottom of another tuff sarcophagus is located in the entrance passage. Excavated [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] include a four-animal [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]], a large iron sword, gilt bronze horse equipment, jewels, and [[Sue ware]] pottry. It is believed to have been built around the mid-6th century in the late Kofun period, and is considered a rare example of a megalithic tomb with clearly excavated artifacts.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a five-minute walk from [[:en:Tatsutagawa Station|Tatsutagawa Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[:en:Ikoma Line|Ikoma Line]].

A moat (approximately 2 meters wide) has been found around the mound.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The horizontal-entry stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] opens to the south of the rear circular mound was built with huge stones. The burial chamber is about 6 meters long, 2.1 meters wide, and 4.4 meters high. The entrance passage is about 8.2 meters long and 2.1 meters wide, with a culvert-type drainage ditch in the center. A house-shaped stone sarcophagus made of [[:en:Tuff|Tuff]] is located in the burial chamber, and the bottom of another tuff sarcophagus is located in the entrance passage. Excavated [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] include a four-animal [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]], a large iron sword, gilt bronze horse equipment, jewels, and [[Sue ware]] pottry. It is believed to have been built around the mid-6th century in the late Kofun period, and is considered a rare example of a megalithic tomb with clearly excavated artifacts.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a five-minute walk from [[:en:Tatsutagawa Station|Tatsutagawa Station]] on the [[:en:Kintetsu Railway|Kintetsu Railway]] [[:en:Ikoma Line|Ikoma Line]].

{{nihongo|''' Udozuka Kofun'''|烏土塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kasugaoka neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Heguri, Nara|en|Heguri, Nara|WD=Q1346790|lt=Heguri, Nara}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1971.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/217109|title=烏土塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Udozuka Kofun is located at the tip of a branching ridge at the eastern foot of the Ikoma Mountains, which run north and south along the border between {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}} and {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were carried out in 1969 . It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, with a total length of 60.5 meters, making it the largest tumulus in the Heguri region. The posterior circular portion has a diameter of about 35 meters and height of about 9 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular portion is 31 meters. The mound was constructed in one tier, although no trace of ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones have been found, the mound had a row of cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}''. A moat (approximately 2 meters wide) has been found around the mound.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The horizontal-entry stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] opens to the south of the rear circular mound was built with huge stones. The burial chamber is about 6 meters long, 2.1 meters wide, and 4.4 meters high. The entrance passage is about 8.2 meters long and 2.1 meters wide, with a culvert-type drainage ditch in the center. A house-shaped stone sarcophagus made of {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff}} is located in the burial chamber, and the bottom of another tuff sarcophagus is located in the entrance passage. Excavated {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} include a four-animal {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}}, a large iron sword, gilt bronze horse equipment, jewels, and [[Sue ware]] pottry. It is believed to have been built around the mid-6th century in the late Kofun period, and is considered a rare example of a megalithic tomb with clearly excavated artifacts.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a five-minute walk from {{ill|Tatsutagawa Station|en|Tatsutagawa Station|WD=Q7688343|lt=Tatsutagawa Station}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|en|Kintetsu Railway|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|Ikoma Line|en|Ikoma Line|WD=Q900218|lt=Ikoma Line}}.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Udozuka Kofun]][[en:Udozuka Kofun]][[ja:烏土塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Udozuka Kofun]][[en:Udozuka Kofun]][[ja:烏土塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Udozuka Kofun]][[en:Udozuka Kofun]][[ja:烏土塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q60988845]] Wikidata name: Udozuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:13, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Udozuka Kofun | native_name =烏土塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Udozuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Udozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Udozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.617797|N|135.702408|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Heguri, Nara|en|Heguri, Nara|WD=Q1346790|lt=Heguri, Nara}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|37|4.07|N|135|42|8.67|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|''' Udozuka Kofun'''|烏土塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kasugaoka neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Heguri, Nara|en|Heguri, Nara|WD=Q1346790|lt=Heguri, Nara}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1971.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/217109|title=烏土塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Udozuka Kofun is located at the tip of a branching ridge at the eastern foot of the Ikoma Mountains, which run north and south along the border between {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}} and {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were carried out in 1969 . It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, with a total length of 60.5 meters, making it the largest tumulus in the Heguri region. The posterior circular portion has a diameter of about 35 meters and height of about 9 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular portion is 31 meters. The mound was constructed in one tier, although no trace of ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones have been found, the mound had a row of cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}''. A moat (approximately 2 meters wide) has been found around the mound.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The horizontal-entry stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] opens to the south of the rear circular mound was built with huge stones. The burial chamber is about 6 meters long, 2.1 meters wide, and 4.4 meters high. The entrance passage is about 8.2 meters long and 2.1 meters wide, with a culvert-type drainage ditch in the center. A house-shaped stone sarcophagus made of {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff}} is located in the burial chamber, and the bottom of another tuff sarcophagus is located in the entrance passage. Excavated {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} include a four-animal {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}}, a large iron sword, gilt bronze horse equipment, jewels, and [[Sue ware]] pottry. It is believed to have been built around the mid-6th century in the late Kofun period, and is considered a rare example of a megalithic tomb with clearly excavated artifacts.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a five-minute walk from {{ill|Tatsutagawa Station|en|Tatsutagawa Station|WD=Q7688343|lt=Tatsutagawa Station}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|en|Kintetsu Railway|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|Ikoma Line|en|Ikoma Line|WD=Q900218|lt=Ikoma Line}}.
<gallery> Udozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial Chamber Udozuka Kofun, genshitsu-3.jpg|Burial chamber looking towards entrance 烏土塚古墳出土 巫女埴輪.JPG| haniwa in form of a woman 烏土塚古墳 出土品.JPG|grave goods </gallery>

{{nihongo|''' Udozuka Kofun'''|烏土塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kasugaoka neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Heguri, Nara|en|Heguri, Nara|WD=Q1346790|lt=Heguri, Nara}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1971.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/217109|title=烏土塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Udozuka Kofun is located at the tip of a branching ridge at the eastern foot of the Ikoma Mountains, which run north and south along the border between {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}} and {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture}}. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were carried out in 1969 . It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, with a total length of 60.5 meters, making it the largest tumulus in the Heguri region. The posterior circular portion has a diameter of about 35 meters and height of about 9 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular portion is 31 meters. The mound was constructed in one tier, although no trace of ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones have been found, the mound had a row of cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}''. A moat (approximately 2 meters wide) has been found around the mound.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The horizontal-entry stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] opens to the south of the rear circular mound was built with huge stones. The burial chamber is about 6 meters long, 2.1 meters wide, and 4.4 meters high. The entrance passage is about 8.2 meters long and 2.1 meters wide, with a culvert-type drainage ditch in the center. A house-shaped stone sarcophagus made of {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff}} is located in the burial chamber, and the bottom of another tuff sarcophagus is located in the entrance passage. Excavated {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} include a four-animal {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}}, a large iron sword, gilt bronze horse equipment, jewels, and [[Sue ware]] pottry. It is believed to have been built around the mid-6th century in the late Kofun period, and is considered a rare example of a megalithic tomb with clearly excavated artifacts.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a five-minute walk from {{ill|Tatsutagawa Station|en|Tatsutagawa Station|WD=Q7688343|lt=Tatsutagawa Station}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|en|Kintetsu Railway|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|Ikoma Line|en|Ikoma Line|WD=Q900218|lt=Ikoma Line}}.
<gallery> Udozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial Chamber Udozuka Kofun, genshitsu-3.jpg|Burial chamber looking towards entrance 烏土塚古墳出土 巫女埴輪.JPG| haniwa in form of a woman 烏土塚古墳 出土品.JPG|grave goods </gallery>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|WD=Q17050860|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]]{{template-inline}} *[https://www.town.heguri.nara.jp/soshiki/14/1081.html Heguri Town home page]{{in lang|ja}} *[https://www.library.pref.nara.jp/nara_2010/0097.html Nara Prefectural Library]{{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Udozuka Kofun]][[en:Udozuka Kofun]][[ja:烏土塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Udozuka Kofun]][[en:Udozuka Kofun]][[ja:烏土塚古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Udozuka Kofun]][[en:Udozuka Kofun]][[ja:烏土塚古墳]]
{{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Udozuka Kofun | native_name =烏土塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Udozuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Udozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Udozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.617797|N|135.702408|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Heguri, Nara|en|Heguri, Nara|WD=Q1346790|lt=Heguri, Nara|1=Heguri, Nara}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|37|4.07|N|135|42|8.67|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|''' Udozuka Kofun'''|烏土塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kasugaoka neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Heguri, Nara|en|Heguri, Nara|WD=Q1346790|lt=Heguri, Nara|1=Heguri, Nara}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1971.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/217109|title=烏土塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Udozuka Kofun is located at the tip of a branching ridge at the eastern foot of the Ikoma Mountains, which run north and south along the border between {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}} and {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were carried out in 1969 . It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, with a total length of 60.5 meters, making it the largest tumulus in the Heguri region. The posterior circular portion has a diameter of about 35 meters and height of about 9 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular portion is 31 meters. The mound was constructed in one tier, although no trace of ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones have been found, the mound had a row of cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}''. A moat (approximately 2 meters wide) has been found around the mound.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The horizontal-entry stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] opens to the south of the rear circular mound was built with huge stones. The burial chamber is about 6 meters long, 2.1 meters wide, and 4.4 meters high. The entrance passage is about 8.2 meters long and 2.1 meters wide, with a culvert-type drainage ditch in the center. A house-shaped stone sarcophagus made of {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff|1=Tuff}} is located in the burial chamber, and the bottom of another tuff sarcophagus is located in the entrance passage. Excavated {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} include a four-animal {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}, a large iron sword, gilt bronze horse equipment, jewels, and [[Sue ware]] pottry. It is believed to have been built around the mid-6th century in the late Kofun period, and is considered a rare example of a megalithic tomb with clearly excavated artifacts.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a five-minute walk from {{ill|Tatsutagawa Station|en|Tatsutagawa Station|WD=Q7688343|lt=Tatsutagawa Station|1=Tatsutagawa Station}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|en|Kintetsu Railway|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|Ikoma Line|en|Ikoma Line|WD=Q900218|lt=Ikoma Line|1=Ikoma Line}}.
<gallery> Udozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial Chamber Udozuka Kofun, genshitsu-3.jpg|Burial chamber looking towards entrance 烏土塚古墳出土 巫女埴輪.JPG| haniwa in form of a woman 烏土塚古墳 出土品.JPG|grave goods </gallery>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|WD=Q17050860|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)}}

{{Short description|Kofun period keyhole-shaped Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Udozuka Kofun | native_name =烏土塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Udozuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Udozuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Udozuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.617797|N|135.702408|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Heguri, Nara|en|Heguri, Nara|WD=Q1346790|lt=Heguri, Nara|1=Heguri, Nara}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|37|4.07|N|135|42|8.67|E}}}}}}
{{nihongo|''' Udozuka Kofun'''|烏土塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]], located in the Kasugaoka neighborhood of the town of {{ill|Heguri, Nara|en|Heguri, Nara|WD=Q1346790|lt=Heguri, Nara|1=Heguri, Nara}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1971.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/217109|title=烏土塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2024}}</ref>
==Overview== The Udozuka Kofun is located at the tip of a branching ridge at the eastern foot of the Ikoma Mountains, which run north and south along the border between {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture|1=Osaka Prefecture}} and {{ill|Nara Prefecture|en|Nara Prefecture|WD=Q131287|lt=Nara Prefecture|1=Nara Prefecture}}. [[Archaeological excavation]]s were carried out in 1969 . It is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, with a total length of 60.5 meters, making it the largest tumulus in the Heguri region. The posterior circular portion has a diameter of about 35 meters and height of about 9 meters, and the width of the anterior rectangular portion is 31 meters. The mound was constructed in one tier, although no trace of ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones have been found, the mound had a row of cylindrical ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}''. A moat (approximately 2 meters wide) has been found around the mound.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The horizontal-entry stone [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] opens to the south of the rear circular mound was built with huge stones. The burial chamber is about 6 meters long, 2.1 meters wide, and 4.4 meters high. The entrance passage is about 8.2 meters long and 2.1 meters wide, with a culvert-type drainage ditch in the center. A house-shaped stone sarcophagus made of {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|WD=Q484924|lt=Tuff|1=Tuff}} is located in the burial chamber, and the bottom of another tuff sarcophagus is located in the entrance passage. Excavated {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} include a four-animal {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}, a large iron sword, gilt bronze horse equipment, jewels, and [[Sue ware]] pottry. It is believed to have been built around the mid-6th century in the late Kofun period, and is considered a rare example of a megalithic tomb with clearly excavated artifacts.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The tumulus is about a five-minute walk from {{ill|Tatsutagawa Station|en|Tatsutagawa Station|WD=Q7688343|lt=Tatsutagawa Station|1=Tatsutagawa Station}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|en|Kintetsu Railway|WD=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|Ikoma Line|en|Ikoma Line|WD=Q900218|lt=Ikoma Line|1=Ikoma Line}}.
<gallery> Udozuka Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial Chamber Udozuka Kofun, genshitsu-3.jpg|Burial chamber looking towards entrance 烏土塚古墳出土 巫女埴輪.JPG| haniwa in form of a woman 烏土塚古墳 出土品.JPG|grave goods </gallery>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|WD=Q17050860|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
